confidence_rationale: This is an academic lecture/Grand Rounds presentation on surgical training innovations and education at Baylor College of Medicine. It contains slides with text, speaker, diagrams, and reference materials about training methodologies, simulation tools, 3D models, assessment instruments (SIMPL), wellness curricula, and virtual education initiatives. There is no FLS task performance visible.
cannot_determine: No FLS task is being performed in this video

verbal_cues: Lecture discussing traditional vs. modern surgical training paradigms
References to FLS program, laparoscopic/robotic simulation, dry-lab models, and VR/AR simulators as training tools
Discussion of structured assessment tools including SIMPL for operative autonomy tracking
common_errors_addressed: Insufficient operative experience due to work-hour restrictions and litigation concerns
Need to decompose complex surgeries into component skills for deliberate practice
Importance of real-time, constructive feedback mechanisms
teaching_content.content_summary: Educational presentation on the evolution of surgical training from apprenticeship model to modern simulation-based, competency-driven education. Topics include innovations in open, endoscopic, laparoscopic, and robotic training modalities; cost-effective 3D-printed models; assessment tools; resident wellness initiatives; and virtual education platforms (virtual visiting electives, Twitter-based learning). Presented as an academic Grand Rounds lecture at Baylor College of Medicine Department of Urology.

video_type_notes: This video is educational/instructional content with narration by a speaker, text-based slides, and academic references. It is not a performance of any FLS task and therefore does not receive an FLS score.
